faqs.question: How can I tell if I have carpenter ants versus regular ants?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are significantly larger than common ants (up to ½ inch long) and are typically black or dark brown. Unlike termites, they don't eat wood but excavate it to build nests, leaving behind piles of sawdust-like material called frass. You may also hear rustling sounds inside walls or see winged ants (swarmers) in spring, indicating an established colony.

faqs.question: How long does carpenter ant treatment take in Paramus homes?

We provide follow-up inspections to ensure all colonies are destroyed and offer preventative treatments to keep your Paramus home protected year-round.
